factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, supported by favorable pricing and higher volumes.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: The supply chain is improving, with normalization of lead times and no significant disruptions reported.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is stable, with the company aligning production with demand.

evidence.text: Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, off-highway, power generation and general industrial markets. Demand in these markets tends to fluctuate in response to overall economic conditions.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with no significant risks of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are stable, with no notable incidents or recalls reported.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs and restrictions are negatively affecting sales and supply chains, particularly in certain regions.

evidence.text: Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date hereof. Future factors that could affect the outcome of forward-looking statements include the following: [...] • changes in international, national and regional trade laws, regulations and policies;
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance, but has maintained a cautious tone regarding future risks.

evidence.text: Shareholders, potential investors and other readers are urged to consider these factors carefully in evaluating the forward-looking statements and are cautioned not to place undue reliance on such forward-looking statements. The forward-looking statements made herein are made only as of the date of this quarterly report and we undertake no obligation to publicly update any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Market share is stable, with the company maintaining its position in key segments.

evidence.text: We have long-standing relationships with many of the leading manufacturers in the markets we serve, including PACCAR Inc, Traton Group, Daimler Trucks North America and Stellantis N.V.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying, with increased global competition and new entrants in emerging markets.

evidence.text: • increasing competition, including increased global competition among our customers in emerging markets;

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are strong, particularly in technology and market leadership.

evidence.text: We are a global power solutions leader comprised of five business segments - Components, Engine, Distribution, Power Systems and Accelera - supported by our global manufacturing and extensive service and support network, skilled workforce and vast technical expertise.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is stable, with the company serving a diverse range of customers.

evidence.text: We have long-standing relationships with many of the leading manufacturers in the markets we serve, including PACCAR Inc, Traton Group, Daimler Trucks North America and Stellantis N.V.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is not explicitly mentioned, but the company is managing compensation expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with no active or potential strike risks reported.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: Talent retention is a focus, with challenges in attracting and retaining critical personnel.

evidence.text: • challenging markets for talent and ability to attract, develop and retain key personnel;

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are contributing to higher demand, particularly in on-highway truck markets.

evidence.text: Worldwide revenues increased by 2 percent in the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to increased power generation demand and higher demand in North American on-highway truck markets, partially offset by decreased sales due to the divestiture of Atmus.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting end-user demand.

evidence.text: We serve our customers through a service network of approximately 450 wholly-owned, joint venture and independent distributor locations and more than 19,000 Cummins certified dealer locations in approximately 190 countries and territories.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on new technologies, particularly in hydrogen production and electrified power systems.

evidence.text: The Accelera segment is currently in the early stages of commercializing these technologies with efforts primarily focused on the development of our electrolyzers for hydrogen production and electrified power systems and related components and subsystems.

factors.summary: Digital technologies and automation are being adopted to drive efficiency and competitive advantages.

evidence.text: Our operations are supported by our global manufacturing and extensive service and support network, skilled workforce and vast technical expertise.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are creating headwinds through higher debt service costs.

evidence.text: • increasing interest rates;

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are a significant factor, impacting operations in numerous countries.

evidence.text: Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power across multiple segments, as evidenced by consistent increases in gross margin and EBITDA. This pricing strength is supported by favorable market conditions and higher demand, though some offsetting factors like divestitures and increased expenses were noted.

evidence.text: The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes.
Distribution segment EBITDA [...] increased $15 million [...] primarily due to favorable pricing.
Power Systems segment EBITDA [...] increased $100 million [...] mainly due to favorable pricing.
The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across multiple segments, driven by increased demand in key markets such as North America and China, as well as higher demand in on-highway truck and power generation markets. This growth is evident across medium-duty, heavy-duty, and light-duty truck markets, with North American sales showing significant increases.

evidence.text: Unit shipments by engine classification...Heavy-duty 37,500...Medium-duty 79,600...Light-duty 57,200

North American sales increased $104 million [...] Power generation sales increased $133 million [...] North American sales increased $132 million [...] North American demand for medium-duty trucks is expected to remain strong.

Net sales in the U.S. and Canada improved 4 percent primarily due to higher demand in on-highway truck markets and power generation markets, partially offset by lower sales due to the divestiture of Atmus.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in key segments like Distribution and Power Systems, driven by favorable pricing and higher volumes. However, this growth is partially offset by divestitures, resulting in a stable overall organic growth rate.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $45 million for the three months ended June 30, 2024, and remained flat as a percentage of net sales versus the comparable period in 2023.
Distribution segment sales for the six months ended June 30, 2024, increased $363 million [...] Power Systems segment sales for the six months ended June 30, 2024, increased $178 million [...]

Worldwide revenues increased by 2 percent in the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to increased power generation demand and higher demand in North American on-highway truck markets, partially offset by decreased sales due to the divestiture of Atmus.

factors.summary: The overall demand trends are mixed, with evidence indicating acceleration in certain segments and slight increases in others, though there are signs of stability and offsetting factors.

evidence.text: Overall demand appears to be accelerating, particularly in key markets like North America, Europe, and Asia.
Distribution segment sales increased 9 percent principally due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially in North America and Europe.
Distribution segment sales for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased $234 million versus the comparable period in 2023.
Power Systems segment sales for the three months ended June 30, 2024, increased $132 million versus the comparable period in 2023.

Worldwide revenues increased by 2 percent in the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to increased power generation demand and higher demand in North American on-highway truck markets, partially offset by decreased sales due to the divestiture of Atmus.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining, particularly in material costs, which has positively impacted margins. The company has been able to offset some costs through favorable pricing and higher productivity.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased 0.2 points as a percentage of sales...partially offset by higher compensation expenses, the divestiture of Atmus and increased product coverage.
The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure, but remain manageable with compensation expenses under control.

evidence.text: Compensation and related expenses included salaries, fringe benefits and variable compensation.

Continued increases in labor costs, as well as other inflationary pressures, could negatively impact earnings. [...] Higher compensation expenses were cited as a factor impacting EBITDA in both segments.
The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by the divestiture of Atmus, higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: The company's margins are showing mixed performance. While gross margins are expanding due to favorable pricing and cost management, some segments face pressure from higher labor and compensation expenses, partially offsetting overall improvements.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased $45 million...Gross margin increased 0.2 points as a percentage of sales.
Segment EBITDA as a percentage of total sales for Distribution decreased slightly from 11.5% to 11.1% [...] However, higher compensation expenses and other costs are impacting margin performance.

Net income attributable to Cummins Inc. was $726 million [...] The increase in gross margin was primarily due to favorable pricing and higher volumes, partially offset by [...] higher compensation expenses, and increased product coverage.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are driving demand, particularly in North America and Europe, with some growth also seen in China.

evidence.text: Construction activity is creating growing demand, particularly in North America and Europe.
Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand for the company's products.
Power Systems segment sales increased 9 percent primarily due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially in North America and China.

Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, off-highway, power generation and general industrial markets. Demand in these markets tends to fluctuate in response to overall economic conditions.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is mixed, with strong growth in power generation across North America, Europe, and Asia, as well as in the Data Center and Commercial markets, particularly in North America and China. However, slower growth is observed in Europe and China, and there has been a decrease in sales due to the divestiture of Atmus, partially offsetting the positive momentum.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales increased 9 percent principally due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially in North America and Europe.

Power generation markets, especially data center markets, are driving sales growth in both Distribution and Power Systems segments. [...] North American demand for medium-duty trucks is expected to remain strong.

Worldwide revenues increased by 2 percent in the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to increased power generation demand and higher demand in North American on-highway truck markets, partially offset by decreased sales due to the divestiture of Atmus.

factors.summary: The aftermarket service revenue is growing positively, driven by strong demand in Engine and Power Systems businesses, as well as a larger installed base and recurring revenue opportunities. However, there is a slight decrease in international sales, partially offset by higher demand across distribution product lines and increased demand for power generation products, particularly in China.

evidence.text: Segment EBITDA as a percentage of total sales 14.1% 14.2% (0.1)
We anticipate demand in our aftermarket business will continue to be robust, driven primarily by strong demand in our Engine and Power Systems businesses.

The slight decrease in international sales was primarily due to decreased sales resulting from the divestiture of Atmus, mostly offset with higher demand across most Distribution product lines and increased demand for power generation products (especially in China).

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is increasing, with a debt to capital ratio rising from 40.3% to 41.5%. This increase is driven by higher debt levels and additional interest expenses resulting from Settlement Agreements. While the balance sheet remains strong with solid credit ratings, the rising debt could potentially impact financial flexibility and expose the company to higher interest costs.

evidence.text: Total debt as a percent of total capital increased from 40.3% to 41.5%
The financial implications resulting from our Settlement Agreements will result in incremental interest expense for debt utilized in funding the civil penalty.

Our debt to capital ratio (total capital defined as debt plus equity) at June 30, 2024, was 41.5 percent, compared to 40.3 percent at December 31, 2023. The increase was primarily due to higher debt balances at June 30, 2024.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is posing significant challenges, including substantial costs and operational constraints. The company has incurred a $2.0 billion charge related to emissions certification and faces ongoing costs and potential future claims.

evidence.text: Environmental regulations are creating significant compliance costs and operational constraints.

While we have reached Settlement Agreements with the EPA, CARB, the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice and the California Attorney General's Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., we have incurred, and likely will incur, other additional claims, costs and expenses in connection with the matters covered by the Settlement Agreements and other matters related to our compliance with emission standards for our engines, including with respect to additional regulatory action and collateral litigation related to these matters.

We recorded a charge of $2.0 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023 to resolve the matters addressed by the Settlement Agreements involving approximately one million of our pick-up truck applications in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

evidence.text: Future factors that could affect the outcome of forward-looking statements include [...] • increased scrutiny from regulatory agencies, as well as unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world; [...] • future bans or limitations on the use of diesel-powered products;
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation appears mixed, with conflicting trends observed across different periods and factors.

evidence.text: Free cash flow generation is strong, supporting various corporate activities.
Net cash used in operating activities increased $1.6 billion for the six months ended June 30, 2024, primarily due to higher working capital requirements of $1.6 billion.

The higher working capital requirements resulted in a cash outflow of $2.5 billion compared to a cash outflow of $849 million in the comparable period of 2023, mainly due to $1.9 billion of payments required by the Settlement Agreements.
We used $575 million in cash from operations for the six months ended June 30, 2024, compared to generating $978 million for the comparable period in 2023.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation exposure primarily related to emissions compliance. This exposure includes past and potential future claims, costs, and expenses, including a $2.0 billion charge associated with Settlement Agreements reached in December 2023, with payments made in the second quarter of 2024.

evidence.text: While we have reached Settlement Agreements with the EPA, CARB, the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice and the California Attorney General's Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., we have incurred, and likely will incur, other additional claims, costs and expenses in connection with the matters covered by the Settlement Agreements and other matters related to our compliance with emission standards for our engines, including with respect to additional regulatory action and collateral litigation related to these matters.

In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle [...] to resolve certain regulatory civil claims [...] We recorded a charge of $2.0 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023 to resolve the matters addressed by the Settlement Agreements.

In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the California Air Resources Board (CARB), the Environmental and Natural Resources Division of the U.S. Department of Justice and the California Attorney General’s Office to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines primarily used in pick-up truck applications in the U.S., which became final and effective in April 2024 (collectively, the Settlement Agreements). In the second quarter of 2024, we made $1.9 billion of payments required by the Settlement Agreements.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is positive, with planned increases in spending, particularly in North America, indicating confidence in future growth. While the overview section mentions stability in capital expenditure, the management's assessment and specific capital expenditures subsection provide clearer evidence of growth-oriented investments.

evidence.text: We believe our access to capital markets, our existing cash and marketable securities, operating cash flow and revolving credit facilities provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund targeted capital expenditures, dividend payments, debt service obligations, projected pension obligations, common stock repurchases and fund joint venture contributions and acquisitions through 2024 and beyond.
We plan to spend an estimated $1.2 billion to $1.3 billion in 2024 on capital expenditures with over 65 percent of these expenditures expected to be invested in North America.

Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost actions, particularly through the divestiture of Atmus, which have resulted in measurable savings. While the company has not explicitly mentioned specific cost reduction actions beyond this, its financial flexibility indicates efficient resource use, supporting its operational and financial goals.

evidence.text: The increases in net income attributable to Cummins Inc. and earnings per diluted share were driven by lower expenses resulting from the divestiture of Atmus and improved gross margin, partially offset by lower equity, royalty and interest income from investees and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in the Chinese renminbi and Mexican peso).

We believe our access to capital markets, our existing cash and marketable securities, operating cash flow and revolving credit facilities provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund targeted capital expenditures, dividend payments, debt service obligations, projected pension obligations, common stock repurchases and fund joint venture contributions and acquisitions through 2024 and beyond.

factors.summary: The company is prioritizing shareholder returns through consistent dividend payments and dividend hikes, while there has been no recent stock buyback activity.

evidence.text: We believe our access to capital markets, our existing cash and marketable securities, operating cash flow and revolving credit facilities provide us with the financial flexibility needed to fund targeted capital expenditures, dividend payments, debt service obligations, projected pension obligations, common stock repurchases and fund joint venture contributions and acquisitions through 2024 and beyond.

We paid dividends of $469 million during the six months ended June 30, 2024. [...] In July 2024, the Board authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend [...] We did not make any repurchases of common stock in the first six months of 2024.
In July 2024, the Board authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of approximately 8 percent from $1.68 per share to $1.82 per share.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Power Generation and Data Center markets, as well as Distribution products. However, there is a slight decrease in international sales due to lower demand in certain regions, partially offset by higher demand in others.

evidence.text: Sales for our Power Systems segment by product line were as follows: [...] Power generation sales increased significantly, driven by higher demand in data center markets. [...] North American sales increased primarily due to higher demand in power generation markets, especially data center and commercial markets.

International demand (excludes the U.S. and Canada) declined 2 percent primarily due to lower sales in Europe and China, partially offset with higher sales in Asia Pacific and Latin America. The slight decrease in international sales was primarily due to decreased sales resulting from the divestiture of Atmus, mostly offset with higher demand across most Distribution product lines and increased demand for power generation products (especially in China).

factors.summary: Management's confidence appears mixed. While they have taken positive actions such as increasing dividends and maintaining credit ratings, they also exhibit caution due to expected weakening demand for heavy-duty trucks and rising labor costs.

evidence.text: We expect demand for heavy-duty trucks in North America to weaken modestly in the second half of 2024. [...] Continued increases in labor costs, as well as other inflationary pressures, could negatively impact earnings.

In July 2024, the Board authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend of approximately 8 percent from $1.68 per share to $1.82 per share. As of the date of this filing, our credit ratings and outlooks from the credit rating agencies remain unchanged.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include demand in North America and China, labor costs, and geopolitical risks.

evidence.text: We expect demand for medium-duty trucks in North America to remain strong. [...] We expect demand for trucks in China to remain stable or improve in 2024. [...] Continued increases in labor costs [...] could negatively impact earnings. [...] The financial implications resulting from our Settlement Agreements will result in incremental interest expense for debt utilized in funding the civil penalty.
Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations impacted international sales by 2 percent (primarily the Chinese renminbi and South African rand). [...] • increasing interest rates;

factors.summary: The company faces a mix of near-term risks, including weakening demand for heavy-duty trucks, rising labor costs, financial impacts from Settlement Agreements, geopolitical instability, economic downturns, and regulatory changes that could affect its sales and supply chains.

evidence.text: We expect demand for heavy-duty trucks in North America to weaken modestly in the second half of 2024. [...] Continued increases in labor costs [...] could negatively impact earnings. [...] The financial implications resulting from our Settlement Agreements will result in incremental interest expense for debt utilized in funding the civil penalty.

Our operations are also affected by geopolitical risks, currency fluctuations, political and economic uncertainty, public health crises (epidemics or pandemics) and regulatory matters, including adoption and enforcement of environmental and emission standards, in the countries we serve.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic focus on growth in Power Generation and Data Center markets, supported by investments in new product development and joint ventures. The strategy emphasizes electrification and alternative power technologies, with significant capital expenditures planned for 2024, particularly in North America.

evidence.text: The company's long-term strategic direction is focused on growth in Power Generation and Data Center markets, supported by investments in new product development and joint ventures.
We expect demand within our Power Systems business to remain strong, including the power generation and mining markets.
The joint venture will manufacture battery cells for electric commercial vehicles and industrial applications.
We plan to spend an estimated $1.2 billion to $1.3 billion in 2024 on capital expenditures with over 65 percent of these expenditures expected to be invested in North America.

factors.summary: The economic conditions are mixed, characterized by strong demand in certain markets, such as medium-duty trucks in North America, but also face risks including potential recessions and increased financial obligations from settlement agreements.

evidence.text: Economic conditions are mixed, with strong demand in certain markets offset by potential recession risks.

Our financial performance depends, in large part, on varying conditions in the markets we serve, particularly the on-highway, off-highway, power generation and general industrial markets. Demand in these markets tends to fluctuate in response to overall economic conditions.

factors.summary: The company has multiple near-term catalysts driving growth, including favorable pricing, increased demand in key markets, successful integration of new products and joint ventures, and the commercialization of new technologies such as hydrogen production and electrified power systems. These initiatives are expected to contribute significantly to future performance, particularly through the joint venture's battery cell manufacturing and the development of electrolyzers.

evidence.text: Favorable pricing is a key driver of EBITDA growth in both segments.
Higher demand in data center and commercial markets is driving sales growth.
The joint venture will manufacture battery cells for electric commercial vehicles and industrial applications, with a significant contribution expected by the end of 2028.

The Accelera segment is currently in the early stages of commercializing these technologies with efforts primarily focused on the development of our electrolyzers for hydrogen production and electrified power systems and related components and subsystems.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active M&A activity, including the formation of a joint venture to expand battery cell production in the U.S. and the divestiture of Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. These actions indicate a strategic focus on both growth and portfolio optimization.

evidence.text: In September 2023, our Accelera business signed an agreement to form a joint venture [...] to accelerate and localize battery cell production and the battery supply chain in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Amplify Cell Technologies LLC Joint Venture

evidence.text: On March 18, 2024, we completed the divestiture of our remaining 80.5 percent ownership of Atmus Filtration Technologies Inc. (Atmus) common stock through a tax-free split-off.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive traction with newly launched products, particularly in Power Generation and battery technologies, as well as in hydrogen production and electrified power systems, which are in the early stages of commercialization. However, there is a slight inconsistency in the focus areas mentioned across different segments, with Power Systems highlighting battery cells for electric vehicles and industrial applications, while the Overview emphasizes hydrogen production and electrified power systems.

evidence.text: Sales for our Power Systems segment by product line were as follows: [...] Power generation sales increased significantly, driven by higher demand in data center markets. [...] The joint venture will manufacture battery cells for electric commercial vehicles and industrial applications.

The Accelera segment is currently in the early stages of commercializing these technologies with efforts primarily focused on the development of our electrolyzers for hydrogen production and electrified power systems and related components and subsystems.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed foreign exchange impacts, with favorable movements in the British pound contributing positively to cash flows, while unfavorable fluctuations in the Chinese renminbi and South African rand negatively affected international sales.

evidence.text: The effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents for the six months ended June 30, 2024, versus the comparable period in 2023, increased $53 million primarily due to favorable fluctuations in the British pound.
Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations impacted international sales by 2 percent (primarily the Chinese renminbi and South African rand).

factors.summary: The company faces significant challenges in meeting environmental and social governance (ESG) commitments, including substantial costs and regulatory hurdles, particularly related to emissions compliance. Additionally, there are risks associated with failing to meet ESG expectations or achieving their ESG goals.

evidence.text: We recorded a charge of $2.0 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023 to resolve the matters addressed by the Settlement Agreements involving approximately one million of our pick-up truck applications in the U.S. [...] The joint venture will manufacture battery cells for electric commercial vehicles and industrial applications.
• failure to meet environmental, social and governance (ESG) expectations or standards, or achieve our ESG goals;

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly in environmental and emissions standards, are creating significant costs and operational challenges. These changes include increased scrutiny from regulatory agencies, unpredictability in the adoption and enforcement of emission standards, and potential future bans or limitations on the use of diesel-powered products. The company has also incurred substantial financial charges related to resolving regulatory civil claims regarding emissions certification and compliance processes.

evidence.text: In December 2023, we announced that we reached an agreement in principle [...] to resolve certain regulatory civil claims regarding our emissions certification and compliance process [...] We recorded a charge of $2.0 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023 to resolve the matters addressed by the Settlement Agreements.

Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date hereof. Future factors that could affect the outcome of forward-looking statements include the following: [...] • increased scrutiny from regulatory agencies, as well as unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world; [...] • future bans or limitations on the use of diesel-powered products;
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.  Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION
